Feature Concepts

Product thinking, applied.

Each post identifies a real friction point, proposes a feature with mockups, and maps the business impact.

Rapido1 year ago

Smart Commute Scheduler

Riders repeat the same route daily yet schedule manually every time. Proposed a commute routine — app learns your pattern, nudges you at the right time, pre-fills everything. Included full two-requirement user flow, profile-level schedule settings, and business analysis: stronger demand forecasting, higher retention, greater loyalty.

BigBasket1 year ago

Ripeness Selector

8-minute delivery is fast — but sometimes too fast to enjoy your produce. Proposed a "Ready to Eat / Needs Ripening" toggle on each item at checkout, so users get their avocados exactly when they want them. Simple UX change with clear satisfaction and repeat-order impact.

CRED1 year ago

Expense Tagging & Category Tracker

CRED users transact daily but have no way to understand where money goes. Proposed three layered requirements: tag expenses by category (Daily Fixed, Monthly Fixed, Occasional, Unexpected), colour-coded history view, and period-based category totals — all within the existing CRED Pay flow.

Blue Dart1 year ago

Proactive OTP — Valid for a Day

Blue Dart's OTP arrives when the courier does, valid only 30 minutes. Proposed sending it at dispatch (morning), valid all day — giving recipients time to delegate or prepare. Reduces courier wait time, failed deliveries, and recipient anxiety. Saves time and effort for everyone.

Zomato / Blinkit1 year ago

Near-Expiry Flash Deals — "Too Good to Throw"

India wastes 11.9M tonnes of restaurant food yearly. Proposed a dedicated "Too Good to Throw" tab in Zomato's Explore section and a featured slot in Blinkit — near-expiry items at steep discounts. Included full wins/hurdles/fixes analysis across platform, restaurant, and customer.

Yulu1 year ago

Indicators on EV Bikes

9 out of 10 Yulu riders felt turn indicators were missing for safer navigation. Without signals, other road users can't tell where a slow-moving Yulu is heading — a real safety gap surfaced through direct user research conversations.

Peeko3 days ago

Age-Sync Gift Mode

Parents know the baby catalog. Gifters only know the baby's age. One tap into Gift Mode — enter the age — and the entire catalog reshapes to show only age-appropriate products. No irrelevant clutter, no decision paralysis. Built a full interactive prototype.

Zudio / Westside1 year ago

Size-Coded Hanger System

An in-store retail experience suggestion — shoppers spend too long hunting for their size, inadvertently creating floor chaos. Colour-coded hangers by size: zero-tech, instant impact for shoppers (faster find), staff (easier restocking), and the brand (higher satisfaction, more purchases).

Airbnb1 month ago

Final Call — Group Decision Engine

Wishlists collect options; 389 WhatsApp messages make the actual decision. Designed ranked voting inside Wishlists — friends mark Top 3, a weighted system surfaces a Winner's Circle. Impact: 47% of Airbnb bookings are group trips. Targets Wishlist → Booking conversion with zero-cost viral acquisition of 4–6 new users per trip.

Uber5 months ago

Pickup Confidence Meter

Pre-booked Uber rides still leave users anxious — will the driver actually show up? Proposed a Pickup Reliability indicator using 6 real-time parameters: active drivers nearby, cancellation rate, historical availability, demand-to-supply ratio, lead time, and driver acceptance rate. Transparency builds trust.

Razorpay9 months ago

Bill Split at Checkout

The post-meal "who pays?" moment solved at the source. Let users split the bill at Razorpay-powered checkout — Pay Total, Split Equally, or Custom Split. Three-sided value: Zomato gets higher conversions, users avoid awkward follow-ups, Razorpay gains micro-transactions. "This isn't just a feature. It's a funnel."

LinkedIn Post ↗
Google Maps2 months ago

Personal Safe Route Avoidance

As a woman riding a two-wheeler at night, one uncomfortable stretch changes everything. Proposed a private "Avoid this road for me in future" option — only applied to your routes, not shared. Quietly personal today, potentially impactful for all women travellers at scale — similar to how construction alerts already work.

Splitwise2 months ago

Personal Expense Lane

Tracking solo expenses in Splitwise currently requires creating a private group with yourself — a workaround that feels like a hack. Proposed a built-in Personal Expense Tracker lane in the main grid: no "group of one", just simple personal tracking living naturally alongside shared bills.

OfflynRecent

Booking Closing Date & Time

Experience hosts on Offlyn need to plan ahead — but the app doesn't let them set a booking deadline. Proposed adding a booking closing date and time to every experience listing, giving hosts predictability for preparation and nudging attendees to commit before it's too late.

Side Project 1 month ago

Built frenchhalwai.com — Free, with Google Sheets as Backend

Vibe-coded a full e-commerce website for a local artisanal patisserie — completely free of cost, with Google Sheets acting as the product database backend. Proved that knowing the basics makes AI-assisted coding 10x faster. The site is live at frenchhalwai.com.

Professional Background

The day job that
sharpens the instinct.

I'm Priya Baheti — a product professional with a background in B2B SaaS and Fintech, actively transitioning into D2C and consumer apps.

At Kinara Capital, I owned onboarding end-to-end: redesigning flows that cut drop-offs by 50% and compressing turnaround time from 7 days to 1. That rigour — identify friction, design the fix, measure impact — is the same lens I bring to every post I publish.

I'm an immediate joiner, fully aligned to Australian Business Hours, and based in Bengaluru.
